_comment: Karpathy LLM-Wiki layer 3 — the schema. Documentation is organised into 6 areas (As-Is Process / Risk & Compliance / Client Experience / Innovation / Target Process / IT Architecture), each with sections. Each element type carries a 'template': the named prose blocks every element of that type must have, with format (paragraph/bullets) and length guidance. Word ranges are calibrated to the COB-003 content so conformance flags genuine deviations. The wiki/ markdown pages are layer 2; raw-sources/ is layer 1.

sections.label: Overview
sections.description: The process at a glance — purpose, scope, owners and the headline numbers.

sections.label: Roles & Organisation
sections.description: The teams, functions and individuals involved in the process, and who is responsible for what.

sections.label: Process Steps
sections.description: The ordered steps that make up the process as it runs today, end to end.

sections.label: Exceptions
sections.description: The off-happy-path cases — where the process deviates, stalls or needs manual handling.

sections.label: Pain Points
sections.description: Where the current process hurts — friction, delays and frustrations reported by the people who run it.

sections.label: Metrics
sections.description: The volumes, timings and service levels that quantify how the process performs.

sections.label: Process Gaps
sections.description: Missing or unclear parts of the As-Is picture that need follow-up before it is complete.

sections.label: Controls
sections.description: The checks and safeguards that keep the process within risk appetite and regulatory limits.

sections.label: Regulation
sections.description: The external rules and supervisory requirements the process must comply with.

sections.label: Control Gaps
sections.description: Weaknesses or holes in the control framework that leave the process exposed.

sections.label: Audit Findings
sections.description: Issues raised by internal or external audit, and their remediation status.

sections.label: Channels
sections.description: The channels through which clients reach the process — branch, online, phone, relationship manager.

sections.label: Touchpoints
sections.description: The individual moments where a client touches the process, step by step.

sections.label: Moments of Truth
sections.description: The make-or-break interactions that most shape how clients judge the process.

sections.label: Friction Points
sections.description: Where the client-facing journey is slow, confusing or effortful.

sections.label: Competitor CX
sections.description: How competitor banks and fintechs run the same client journey.

sections.label: CX Benchmarks
sections.description: Industry benchmarks and client-expectation research for this kind of journey.

sections.label: Market Trends
sections.description: Market shifts, regulation and technology trends bearing on the process's future.

sections.label: Competitor Innovation
sections.description: What competitors and fintechs are doing that could reshape this process.

sections.label: Innovation Ideas
sections.description: Concrete ideas for improving or reinventing the process.

sections.label: Prioritisation
sections.description: How the innovation ideas rank by value, effort and feasibility.

sections.label: Innovation Risks
sections.description: The risks that come with pursuing the innovation ideas.

sections.label: TO-BE Process Design
sections.description: The redesigned future-state process, step by step.

sections.label: Transformation Decisions
sections.description: The key decisions that shape the move from the current state to the target.

sections.label: Requirements
sections.description: Testable statements of what the target process and systems must do, derived from the transformation decisions and gaps.

sections.label: Dependencies
sections.description: The target process's boundary — the upstream feeders and downstream consumers it connects to.

sections.label: Assumptions
sections.description: What the target design and the transformation rest on — assumptions still to be confirmed.

sections.label: Gap Resolution
sections.description: How each As-Is gap, pain point and control weakness is resolved in the target state.

sections.label: Validation
sections.description: Evidence and sign-off that the target design holds up.

sections.label: Systems
sections.description: The applications and platforms the process runs on.

sections.label: Integrations
sections.description: The interfaces and data flows that connect those systems.

sections.label: Capabilities
sections.description: Business capabilities the target process needs — the 'what', not the 'how'.

sections.label: Target Applications
sections.description: Applications hosting the target capabilities, with build / buy / configure / keep verdicts.

sections.label: Architecture Decisions
sections.description: ADRs — locked-in technical decisions with their alternatives and consequences.

sections.label: Target Integrations
sections.description: Interfaces between target applications, with patterns (sync / async / event / batch).

sections.label: Components
sections.description: Sub-elements of target applications — services, gateways, workers.

sections.label: NFRs
sections.description: Non-functional requirements with category, target value and traces back to controls and regulation.

sections.label: Migration Phases
sections.description: Staged sequence from today's systems landscape to the target architecture.

raci.note: How this role participates in each process step — the RACI assignment that drives the RACI matrix. Field `raci` — a list of objects, each `{ "step": <process-step id>, "level": <R|A|C|I> }`: R responsible, A accountable, C consulted, I informed. A role carries an entry only for the steps it participates in. Across all roles, every step needs at least one R and exactly one A.

template.heading: Responsibility
template.purpose: What the role owns in the process, stated in one clear sentence.

template.heading: In this process
template.purpose: The specific steps and controls this role performs in this process.

fields.label: Owner
fields.hint: Who performs this step — a role (e.g. 'Operations Analyst'), or 'system / unassigned' if automated. Never a named individual.

fields.label: SLA
fields.hint: The turnaround target for this step (e.g. 'within 2 hours', 'same business day').

fields.label: Condition
fields.hint: Any precondition or entry criterion that must hold before this step runs.

transitions.note: Outgoing flow from this step. Field `transitions` — a list of objects, each `{ "to": <target id>, "kind": <normal|branch|loopback|exception>, "when": <condition> }`: the element it flows to (a process-step or exception id), the transition kind, and the condition under which it fires.

template.heading: What happens
template.purpose: The concrete actions performed in this step, in plain language.

template.heading: Inputs
template.purpose: Each thing the step consumes — documents, data or prior outputs.

template.heading: Outputs
template.purpose: Each thing the step produces and hands to the next step.

template.heading: Why it matters
template.purpose: The risk this step controls or the value it adds.

fields.label: Category
fields.hint: The kind of exception (e.g. data-quality, sanctions hit, missing approval).

fields.label: Frequency
fields.hint: Roughly how often the exception occurs, as a percentage of cases.

fields.label: Impact
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — the severity of the exception's effect on the process or client.

fields.label: Handling
fields.hint: The role accountable for resolving this exception.

template.heading: Description
template.purpose: What the exception is and the conditions under which it arises.

template.heading: Handling
template.purpose: How the exception is resolved, and by whom.

template.heading: Impact
template.purpose: Effect on cycle time, cost, the client or compliance.

fields.label: Type
fields.hint: PREVENTIVE / DETECTIVE / CORRECTIVE — when the control acts relative to the risk.

fields.label: Execution
fields.hint: MANUAL / AUTOMATED / HYBRID — whether a person performs the control, the system enforces it, or both (a manual trigger plus an automated execution, e.g. an analyst initiating an automated screening tool).

fields.label: Effectiveness
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — how well the control mitigates its risk, as assessed.

fields.label: Owner
fields.hint: The accountable function (e.g. 'Compliance', 'Operations') — never a named individual, even for an automated control.

template.heading: What it checks
template.purpose: The specific risk or condition the control verifies.

template.heading: Control activity
template.purpose: How the control is performed, by whom and with what.

template.heading: Risk addressed
template.purpose: The risk that would materialise without this control.

template.heading: Timing
template.purpose: When and how often the control runs.

fields.label: Domain
fields.hint: The regulatory domain (e.g. AML, sanctions, prudential, conduct).

fields.label: As of
fields.hint: The date this regulatory position was captured or last verified.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the rule — the regulation, supervisory guidance or article.

template.heading: What it requires
template.purpose: The obligations the regulation imposes on the process.

template.heading: Why it applies
template.purpose: Why this regulation is in scope for this process.

template.heading: How it is met
template.purpose: The controls and process steps that satisfy the regulation.

fields.label: Severity
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — the regulatory exposure the gap creates.

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: Where the gap stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

template.heading: The gap
template.purpose: What is missing or non-compliant.

template.heading: Risk
template.purpose: The regulatory or operational exposure the gap creates.

template.heading: Remediation
template.purpose: The actions needed to close the gap.

fields.label: Audit
fields.hint: The date the finding was raised by audit.

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: Where the finding stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

fields.label: Severity
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — the audit-assessed severity.

template.heading: Finding
template.purpose: What the audit observed, with enough context to act on.

template.heading: Recommendation
template.purpose: The auditor's recommended action.

fields.label: Category
fields.hint: The kind of pain point (e.g. manual effort, delay, rework, hand-off friction).

fields.label: Severity
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — how badly this hurts the process.

fields.label: Priority
fields.hint: P1 / P2 / P3 — how urgently it should be addressed.

template.heading: Description
template.purpose: What the pain point is, from the staff or process view.

template.heading: Impact
template.purpose: How it slows, costs or degrades the process.

template.heading: Root cause
template.purpose: The underlying reason the pain point exists.

fields.label: Severity
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — how badly this friction hurts the client experience.

template.heading: Description
template.purpose: Where and how the client experiences friction.

template.heading: Root cause
template.purpose: Why the friction occurs.

template.heading: Client impact
template.purpose: How it affects the client's effort and perception.

fields.label: Channel
fields.hint: The channel this touchpoint occurs on (e.g. branch, app, call centre).

fields.label: Client Effort Score
fields.hint: Customer Effort Score for this touchpoint, if measured.

template.heading: What the client does
template.purpose: The client's action at this touchpoint.

template.heading: What the bank does
template.purpose: The bank's response or action at this touchpoint.

template.heading: Experience
template.purpose: How the touchpoint feels — effort, clarity and emotion.

fields.label: Sentiment
fields.hint: positive / negative / critical — the client's emotional read of this moment.

template.heading: The moment
template.purpose: The specific moment in the client journey.

template.heading: Why it matters
template.purpose: Why this moment shapes the client's overall perception.

template.heading: Design implication
template.purpose: What the process should do about this moment.

fields.label: Type
fields.hint: DIGITAL or ASSISTED — self-service, or mediated by staff.

template.heading: Description
template.purpose: What the channel is and how clients reach it.

template.heading: Usage
template.purpose: When and how much the channel is used in onboarding.

fields.label: Competitor
fields.hint: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this competitor CX observation was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the observation.

template.heading: The journey
template.purpose: How a European corporate bank runs this client journey.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: What it means for this process — the experience gap it opens.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The review, case study or announcement that evidences it.

fields.label: Competitor
fields.hint: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this competitor CX observation was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the observation.

template.heading: The journey
template.purpose: How a global corporate bank runs this client journey.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: What it means for this process — the experience gap it opens.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The review, case study or announcement that evidences it.

fields.label: Competitor
fields.hint: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this competitor CX observation was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the observation.

template.heading: The journey
template.purpose: How a fintech runs this client journey.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: What it means for this process — the experience gap it opens.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The review, case study or announcement that evidences it.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this benchmark figure was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the benchmark.

template.heading: The benchmark
template.purpose: The industry CX standard or client expectation, described plainly.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: How this process measures against it.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The specific figure or signal — survey, study or standard.

fields.label: Target
fields.hint: The goal / SLA figure (e.g. 'same business day'). The figure stated in a process-design document is almost always the target, not the measured actual.

fields.label: Value
fields.hint: The measured actual today. Leave empty if only a target is known — an unmeasured metric is itself worth flagging.

fields.label: Trend
fields.hint: The direction of travel — improving, flat or worsening — if known.

template.heading: Definition
template.purpose: What the metric measures and how it is calculated.

template.heading: Current reading
template.purpose: The latest value and the trend.

template.heading: Why it matters
template.purpose: What the metric tells the process owner.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: The area the gap sits in (e.g. As-Is process, controls, systems).

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: Where the gap stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

template.heading: The gap
template.purpose: What is missing in how the process is run or measured.

template.heading: Impact
template.purpose: What the gap prevents or puts at risk.

template.heading: Next step
template.purpose: The recommended action to close the gap.

fields.label: Horizon
fields.hint: regulatory-deadline / near-term / emerging — how soon the trend bites.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this trend was captured or last verified.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL — the market study, analyst report or article.

template.heading: The trend
template.purpose: The market or industry trend, described plainly.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: Why the trend matters for this process.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The specific figure, statistic or signal that evidences the trend — the data point, not a re-listing of the source.

fields.label: Competitor
fields.hint: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this competitor observation was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the observation.

template.heading: The move
template.purpose: The innovation a European corporate bank is pursuing, described plainly.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: What it means for this process — the competitive gap it opens.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The announcement, report or launch that evidences the move.

fields.label: Competitor
fields.hint: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this competitor observation was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the observation.

template.heading: The move
template.purpose: The innovation a global corporate bank is pursuing, described plainly.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: What it means for this process — the competitive gap it opens.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The announcement, report or launch that evidences the move.

fields.label: Competitor
fields.hint: The competitor bank or fintech this observation is about.

fields.label: Sourced
fields.hint: The date this competitor observation was captured.

fields.label: Source
fields.hint: Citation or URL for the observation.

template.heading: The move
template.purpose: The innovation a fintech is pursuing, described plainly.

template.heading: Relevance
template.purpose: What it means for this process — the competitive gap it opens.

template.heading: Evidence
template.purpose: The announcement, report or launch that evidences the move.

fields.label: Category
fields.hint: The kind of idea (e.g. automation, new channel, control redesign).

fields.label: Strategic fit
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — how well the idea aligns with strategy.

fields.label: Complexity
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — the effort and difficulty of delivering it.

template.heading: The idea
template.purpose: The proposed change, described plainly.

template.heading: Expected benefit
template.purpose: The outcome it would deliver and for whom.

template.heading: Feasibility
template.purpose: Effort, dependencies and the main risks.

fields.label: Severity
fields.hint: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE — the severity of this risk if it materialises.

template.heading: The risk
template.purpose: What could go wrong with the innovation.

template.heading: Likelihood & impact
template.purpose: How likely the risk is and how severe its effect.

template.heading: Mitigation
template.purpose: How the risk can be reduced or managed.

template.heading: Target description
template.purpose: How the step or process works in the target state.

template.heading: What changes
template.purpose: Each concrete change from the As-Is.

template.heading: Rationale
template.purpose: Why the target state is designed this way.

fields.label: Type
fields.hint: The kind of decision (e.g. build/buy, scope, sequencing).

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: Where the decision stands — proposed, agreed, or deferred.

template.heading: The decision
template.purpose: What was decided.

template.heading: Options considered
template.purpose: The alternatives that were weighed.

template.heading: Rationale
template.purpose: Why this option was chosen over the others.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: The area this gap was surfaced in during validation.

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: Where the gap stands — open, in remediation, or closed.

template.heading: The gap
template.purpose: The As-Is gap being resolved.

template.heading: Resolution
template.purpose: How the target state closes the gap.

template.heading: Status
template.purpose: Where the resolution currently stands.

fields.label: Type
fields.hint: CORE / SUPPORTING / EXTERNAL — the system's role in this process.

template.heading: Purpose
template.purpose: What the system is for.

template.heading: Role in this process
template.purpose: Which steps use the system and how.

template.heading: What connects
template.purpose: The two systems the integration links.

template.heading: What flows
template.purpose: Each kind of data that moves across the integration.

fields.label: Type
fields.hint: FUNCTIONAL or NON-FUNCTIONAL — what the requirement constrains.

fields.label: MoSCoW
fields.hint: MUST / SHOULD / COULD / WONT — MoSCoW priority for this requirement.

template.heading: Requirement
template.purpose: One testable statement of what the target process or system must do.

template.heading: Rationale
template.purpose: The transformation decision or gap this requirement comes from.

template.heading: Acceptance criteria
template.purpose: How you would confirm the requirement is met.

fields.label: Direction
fields.hint: UPSTREAM / DOWNSTREAM / BIDIRECTIONAL — how this process and the dependency relate.

template.heading: The dependency
template.purpose: The connected process or party at the boundary.

template.heading: What crosses the boundary
template.purpose: The data, trigger or artefact handed over, and in which direction.

template.heading: Why it matters
template.purpose: The impact if the dependency fails or runs late.

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: OPEN / CONFIRMED / INVALIDATED — whether the assumption still holds.

template.heading: The assumption
template.purpose: What is being assumed.

template.heading: Why it is unconfirmed
template.purpose: What is unknown, and why it could not be verified.

template.heading: Impact if wrong
template.purpose: What breaks if the assumption does not hold.

fields.label: Criticality
fields.hint: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW — how essential this capability is to the target process.

fields.label: Reuse
fields.hint: NEW / REUSED — whether this is a fresh capability or one already in the catalog.

fields.label: Owning domain
fields.hint: The business domain that owns this capability (e.g. 'Case management', 'Payments').

template.heading: Description
template.purpose: What the capability does — the function from the consumer's perspective.

template.heading: Inputs and outputs
template.purpose: What the capability needs to operate, and what it produces.

template.heading: Boundaries
template.purpose: What this capability is NOT responsible for — adjacent capabilities it relies on.

fields.label: Verdict
fields.hint: BUILD / BUY / CONFIGURE / KEEP — the disposition of this app in the target state.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.hint: Vendor name for BUY apps; tech stack for BUILD; vendor + 'configured' for CONFIGURE.

fields.label: Owning domain
fields.hint: The business domain that owns this app.

fields.label: Cost band
fields.hint: Build / run / annual licence cost band (rough budgeting estimate).

template.heading: Rationale
template.purpose: Why this app, and why this verdict — what the alternatives were.

template.heading: Tech stack
template.purpose: Concrete technologies, hosting region, deployment model.

template.heading: Risks
template.purpose: Risks introduced or carried by this disposition (vendor lock-in, residency, capacity, etc.).

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: DRAFT / PROPOSED / ACCEPTED / REJECTED / SUPERSEDED — the ADR lifecycle state.

fields.label: Owner
fields.hint: The accountable architect (a role, not a named person).

fields.label: Domain
fields.hint: The architectural domain this decision belongs to (e.g. 'Case management', 'Payments').

template.heading: Context
template.purpose: Why the decision is needed — the situation, constraints and forces in play.

template.heading: Decision
template.purpose: What we decided to do.

template.heading: Alternatives considered
template.purpose: The options we weighed and why each was not chosen.

template.heading: Consequences
template.purpose: What follows from the decision — new dependencies, NFRs, risks, technical debt.

fields.label: Pattern
fields.hint: SYNC / ASYNC / EVENT / BATCH — the interaction style.

fields.label: Direction
fields.hint: ONE-WAY / TWO-WAY / PUB-SUB — the data-flow direction.

fields.label: Contract
fields.hint: Interface contract reference — OpenAPI 3.1, AsyncAPI 2.6, CMIS, Snowflake stage, etc.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Approximate volume + latency target (e.g. '~12k req/day · p95 380ms').

template.heading: Purpose
template.purpose: Why the two apps need to talk, and what data flows.

template.heading: Contract details
template.purpose: Auth, schema, idempotency, versioning — the contract specifics.

template.heading: Failure mode
template.purpose: Behaviour when the downstream is unavailable or returns an error.

fields.label: Tech
fields.hint: The technology / framework (e.g. 'Spring Boot 3', 'Camunda Zeebe', 'Quarkus').

fields.label: Data store
fields.hint: The persistence layer this component owns, if any (e.g. 'Postgres 16').

fields.label: Hosting
fields.hint: Runtime environment (e.g. 'EKS · eu-frankfurt', 'AWS Lambda', 'on-prem').

fields.label: Scaling
fields.hint: Replica / scaling profile (e.g. 'HPA · 3 → 12 replicas').

template.heading: Responsibility
template.purpose: What this component owns within its application.

template.heading: Technical detail
template.purpose: Tech choices, data ownership, scaling profile.

fields.label: Category
fields.hint: PERFORMANCE / AVAILABILITY / SECURITY / COMPLIANCE / SCALABILITY — the NFR family.

fields.label: Target
fields.hint: The concrete, testable target (e.g. 'p95 < 1.2s', 'RTO ≤ 4h', '10 years immutable').

fields.label: Owner
fields.hint: The team accountable for meeting the NFR (a role, not a person).

template.heading: Definition
template.purpose: What is being measured and at what threshold.

template.heading: Measurement
template.purpose: How the target is observed in production — instrumentation and method.

template.heading: Verification
template.purpose: How the NFR is tested before go-live — load test, audit, etc.

fields.label: Status
fields.hint: PLANNED / IN-FLIGHT / DONE — current execution state.

fields.label: Start
fields.hint: Calendar quarter the phase begins (e.g. '2026 Q3').

fields.label: End
fields.hint: Calendar quarter the phase completes.

fields.label: Owner
fields.hint: The accountable delivery owner (a role).

template.heading: Scope
template.purpose: What this phase delivers and what it explicitly does not.

template.heading: Acceptance criteria
template.purpose: What must be true for the phase to be called done.

template.heading: Risks
template.purpose: Risks that could push the phase late or break dependent phases.

_fieldValuesComment: Allowed values for the enumerated frontmatter fields — the single source skills draw on, surfaced by show_template.py. Open-text fields (owner, source, competitor, …) are not listed.
_provenanceComment: Hallucination countermeasure (HALLUCINATION-PLAN.md). Every template-bearing element carries a `provenance` frontmatter key: a JSON object keyed by template heading title, each value {source, evidence}. `source` is one of fieldValues.provenanceSource — elicited (the SME said it; evidence is the verbatim SME quote), document (from an uploaded source; evidence is the source quote), proposed (AI-added, unconfirmed; evidence empty), web (web-sourced, unconfirmed; evidence is URL + snippet + fetch date), legacy-approved (predates this rule; evidence not required). check_conformance.py requires a provenance entry for every template heading, requires non-empty evidence for elicited/document/web, and cross-checks the map keys against the template headings. set_approval.py blocks `approved` while any heading is proposed or web (unconfirmed).
